note: Native-review draft covering the full master corpus (474 equations). `dictation` is a test-corpus utterance for engine localization — native phrasing, faithful to the master `unicodemath` (never the English string). Grouping uses explicit abre/cierra markers (paréntesis, corchete, llave); powers use al cuadrado / al cubo / elevado a; subscripts use sub; fractions use partido por (binomial coefficient uses número combinatorio … sobre …). Capital letters are spelled bare (uve, erre, efe) and marked with a following "mayúscula" only to resolve a same-letter case clash within a formula. See [[dictation-localization-progress]].
